signals.headline: IMF's April 2, 2026, Article IV Consultation projects U
signals.raw: IMF's April 2, 2026, Article IV Consultation projects U.S. GDP at 2.4% for 2026, core PCE inflation at 2.6% by year-end (up from 2.9% in 2025), and minimal Fed rate cuts (fed funds from 3.6% to 3.4%), citing tariffs (7-8.5% effective rate) pushing goods prices higher while services cool[1].
matched_markets: Will Powell say "Tariff inflation" during April press conference?

signals.headline: Powell described a "very unusual" economy in late 2025, noting tariff-driven goods inflation peaking Q1 2026, weakening
signals.raw: Powell described a "very unusual" economy in late 2025, noting tariff-driven goods inflation peaking Q1 2026, weakening labor market (official jobs overstated by ~60k/month), and neutral policy stance[2].
matched_markets: Will Powell say "Tariff inflation" during April press conference?

signals.headline: Earlier Powell remarks (pre-April 2026) emphasized tariffs as one-time price hikes (not persistent inflation), with effe
signals.raw: Earlier Powell remarks (pre-April 2026) emphasized tariffs as one-time price hikes (not persistent inflation), with effects visible but expected to moderate mid-year; average tariff rates hit 17-18.6%, straining Fed's dual mandate[3][4].
matched_markets: Will Powell say "Tariff inflation" during April press conference?
